steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. The underlined portion “(film containing scaled-down reproductions of documents)” sits in parentheses immediately after the word microfilm. The decisive clue is its position and form: it is a parenthetical that directly restates what microfilm is.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "It gives information about a famous person.": The underlined phrase says nothing about Frederick Douglass or any person; it defines a technology term. ✗
  • "It explains the meaning of a word.": The parenthetical defines microfilm for readers who may be unfamiliar with the term, which is exactly “explaining the meaning of a word.” ✓
  • "It describes a debate among historians.": No disagreement or debate appears in the underlined phrase or anywhere nearby in the passage. ✗
  • "It summarizes an unexpected finding.": A parenthetical definition is not a finding, and nothing in the phrase signals surprise or discovery. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
